en.extract:
The yellow-throated leafbird (Chloropsis palawanensis) is a species of bird in the family Chloropseidae.
It is endemic to the Palawan in the Philippines. Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forest. Along with the Philippine leafbird, it is one of the two endemic leafbirds in the country.  


Description and taxonomy
It is slightly smaller than the Philippine leafbird and is generally more colorful with its bright yellow throat and blue lores and edges of its wing. This is monotypic and has no subspecies.


Ecology and behavior
Typically forages alone but also joins mixed-species flocks, often with bulbuls, foraging for fruits and seeds.
No information known about its breeding habits.


Habitat and conservation status
Its habitat is primary lowland forest but it does occasionally visit secondary growth. 
It is assessed as least-concern species under the IUCN. However, Palawan's forests are under threat due to illegal logging, deforestation, land conversion and mining. The whole of Palawan was designated as a Biosphere Reserve; however, protection and enforcement of laws has been difficult and these threats still continue. It occurs in just one protected area in the Iwahig Prison and Penal Farm.

The '''yellow-throated leafbird''' ('''''Chloropsis palawanensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Chloropseidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Palawan]] in the [[Philippines]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest|tropical moist lowland forest]]. Along with the [[Philippine leafbird]], it is one of the two endemic leafbirds in the country.  

== Description and taxonomy ==
It is slightly smaller than the [[Philippine leafbird]] and is generally more colorful with its bright yellow throat and blue lores and edges of its wing.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Ress |first1=Elliott M. |last2=Wells |first2=David |date=2023 |title=Philippine Leafbird (Chloropsis flavipennis), version 2.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/philea1/2.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.philea1.02 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref> This is monotypic and has no subspecies.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Wells |first=David |date=2020 |title=Yellow-throated Leafbird (Chloropsis palawanensis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/yetlea1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.yetlea1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Ecology and behavior ==
Typically forages alone but also joins mixed-species flocks, often with bulbuls, foraging for fruits and seeds.

No information known about its breeding habits.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Wells |first=David |date=2020 |title=Yellow-throated Leafbird (Chloropsis palawanensis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/yetlea1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.yetlea1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
Its habitat is primary lowland forest but it does occasionally visit secondary growth. 

It is assessed as [[least-concern species]] under the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature|IUCN]]. However, [[Palawan]]'s forests are under threat due to [[illegal logging]], deforestation, land conversion and mining. The whole of [[Palawan]] was designated as a Biosphere Reserve; however, protection and enforcement of laws has been difficult and these threats still continue. It occurs in just one protected area in the [[Iwahig Prison and Penal Farm]].<ref>{{Cite report |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22704947/130352344 |title=Chloropsis palawanensis: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2018: e.T22704947A130352344 |last=IUCN |date=2018-08-07 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/iucn.uk.2018-2.rlts.t22704947a130352344.en |language=en}}</ref>
